title: Commersonia madagascariensis
taxonomy: Commersonia madagascariensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Madagascar. This plant is a part of the Commersonia genus, which consists of about 24 species. Commersonia madagascariensis is commonly known as the Madagascar bush-pea. It is an endemic species to the island of Madagascar.

intro: Commersonia madagascariensis, also known as the Madagascar cobbler, is a plant species native to Madagascar and Australia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which can aid in wound healing and potentially treat various infections. The bioactive constituents of Commersonia madagascariensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and infections.
